121	```
122	sending an `X`-scheme `p : Y ⟶ X` to the pushforward along `p` of the pullback
123	`p^* F`. We record here the two *pre-coherence* bricks of `G` — its action on
124	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
125	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
126	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
127	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
128	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
129	`pushPullMap`. -/

131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))

161	/- **The functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`.**
162	Assembling `pushPullObj` / `pushPullMap` into the functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤
163	X.Modules` requires
164	```
165	  pushPullMap_id   : pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y)
166	  pushPullMap_comp : pushPullMap F (g ≫ h) = pushPullMap F h ≫ pushPullMap F g
167	```
168	Both laws are proved axiom-clean below (see `pushPullMap_id` and `pushPullMap_comp`),
169	and `pushPullFunctor` is assembled from them immediately after. -/
170	
171	/-! ### Functor laws of the push–pull functor `G` -/
172	
173	/-- Identity law of the push–pull functor `G`. -/
174	lemma pushPullMap_id (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) :
175	    pushPullMap F (𝟙 Y) = 𝟙 (pushPullObj F Y) := by
176	  -- `star`: the unit-triangle for the identity adjunction `pullback 𝟙 ⊣ pushforward 𝟙`.
177	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := Y.left.Modules))
178	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left))
179	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
180	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
181	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
182	  -- `hru`: right-unitality of the pullback pseudofunctor, applied at `F`.
183	  have hru := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_right_unitality (X := Y.left) (f := Y.hom)
184	  have hru2 := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) hru
185	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app] at hru2
186	  have hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app
187	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
188	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
189	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)))
190	        (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
191	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
192	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))) := by
193	    apply Scheme.Modules.hom_ext
194	    intro U; rfl
195	  -- the unit zig-zag for the identity adjunction collapses on `M`
196	  have hzig : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 Y.left)).unit.app
197	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) ≫
198	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.app
199	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 Y.left)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) ≫
200	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) =
201	      𝟙 ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
202	    have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y.left).hom.naturality
203	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F))
204	    simp only [Functor.id_map] at hnat
205	    erw [← hnat, ← reassoc_of% star]
206	    exact Iso.inv_hom_id_app _ _
207	  -- the pullback comparison + the over-triangle transport collapse via right-unitality
208	  have hib_inner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F ≫
209	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
210	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F) := by
211	    rw [eqToHom_app] at hru2
212	    rw [← hru2, ← Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app]; simp
213	  have hib : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
214	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (𝟙 Y.left) Y.hom).hom.app F) ≫
215	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
216	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
217	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
218	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId Y.left).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)) := by
219	    have he : eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).obj
220	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) (Category.id_comp Y.hom)) =
221	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward Y.hom).map
222	          (eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
223	            (Category.id_comp Y.hom))) := by
224	      rw [eqToHom_map]
225	    rw [he, ← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hib_inner
226	  -- assemble
227	  simp only [pushPullMap, Over.id_left]
228	  erw [reassoc_of% hpf, hib, ← Functor.map_comp]
229	  erw [hzig, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl

232	/-- **Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push–pull head.**
233	For composable scheme morphisms `f : A ⟶ B`, `p : B ⟶ Z` and `N : Z.Modules`, the
234	adjunction unit at `N` for `p` followed by the *head* of `pushPullMap` (the
235	pushforward of the unit for `f`, then the pushforward comparison) equals the unit
236	for the composite `f ≫ p` followed by the pushforward of the inverse pullback
237	comparison. This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
238	`η^{f≫p}` into the iterated units `η^p`, `η^f`; it is the reusable ingredient that
239	the functoriality (pentagon) law of `pushPullMap` repeatedly consumes when
240	splitting a composite unit. Project-local supplement. -/
241	lemma pushPull_unit_mate {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
242	    (N : Z.Modules) :
243	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
244	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
245	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
246	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
247	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
248	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) =
249	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N ≫
250	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
251	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv.app N) := by
252	  have key := unit_conjugateEquiv
253	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).comp
254	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
255	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p))
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).inv N
257	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at key
258	  simpa only [Category.assoc] using key
259	
260	/-- **Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push–pull tail** (kernel-cheap
261	generalised form). The morphism map `pushPullMap` glues its pullback-comparison leg
262	to the target object `pushPullObj F Y₂` by two `eqToHom` coercions along the
263	over-triangle `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`. Cancelling those coercions *in situ*
264	(at the concrete pushforward/pullback objects) provokes a kernel `whnf` blow-up.
265	This lemma states the cancellation **with the over-triangle equality as a free
266	hypothesis** `h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂`, so the proof is a single `subst h` (after which
267	the transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish — kernel-cheap) followed by
268	`simp`. Applying it to `pushPullMap` via `rw` rewrites the tail without forcing the
269	kernel to unfold the comparison objects: the over-triangle leg
270	`eqToHom ≫ (pushforward p₂).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom` collapses to the
271	transport-light `(pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map (pullbackComp).hom ≫ eqToHom`, the
272	single residual `eqToHom` carrying the unavoidable object identification of the
273	codomain `pushPullObj F Y₂`. Reusable pre-coherence brick for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
274	lemma pushPull_transport_cancel {Y₁ Y₂ : Scheme.{u}}
275	    (gl : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (p₁ : Y₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Y₂ ⟶ X)
276	    (h : gl ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
277	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gl).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) h) ≫
279	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
280	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
281	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) =
282	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (gl ≫ p₁)).map
283	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gl p₁).hom.app F) ≫
284	      eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
285	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) h) := by
286	  subst h
287	  simp
288	
289	/-- **Composite-unit decomposition for the push–pull head.** The adjunction unit
290	`η^{f≫p}` for a composite morphism, expressed through the iterated units `η^p`,
291	`η^f` and the pushforward/pullback comparison isomorphisms. This is the
292	`pushPull_unit_mate` identity solved for `η^{f≫p}` (post-composing with
293	`(f≫p)_*(pullbackComp).hom` cancels the `pullbackComp.inv` factor). Reusable brick
294	for the composition law of `pushPullMap`. -/
295	lemma pushPull_unit_comp {A B Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : A ⟶ B) (p : B ⟶ Z)
296	    (N : Z.Modules) :
297	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ p)).unit.app N =
298	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).unit.app N ≫
299	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
300	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
301	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
302	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f p).hom.app
303	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).obj N)) ≫
304	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (f ≫ p)).map
305	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f p).hom.app N) := by
306	  have m := pushPull_unit_mate f p N
307	  erw [reassoc_of% m, ← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app,
308	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
309	
310	/-- The pushforward pseudofunctor is *strict* on sheaves of modules: the
311	`pushforwardComp` comparison `2`-cell is the identity on the nose. Holds by `rfl`
312	(`pushforward (a ≫ p) = pushforward a ⋙ pushforward p` definitionally). Project-local
313	collapse used to discharge the pushforward legs of the push–pull pentagon. -/
314	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_id {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p : Z₁ ⟶ Z₃)
315	    (M : Z₂.Modules) : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p).hom.app M = 𝟙 _ :=
316	  rfl

318	/-- **Scheme-level push–pull comparison map with the over-triangle as a free
319	hypothesis.** This is the body of `pushPullMap` with the underlying scheme map
320	`a`, the two structure maps `p₁ p₂`, and the over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`
321	generalised away from the `Over X` packaging. Spelling the over-triangle as a free
322	hypothesis is what makes the composition law provable by `subst` (after which the
323	`eqToHom` over-triangle transports become `eqToHom rfl = 𝟙` and vanish, dodging the
324	kernel `whnf` wall). By construction `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom
325	Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F` definitionally. Project-local. -/
326	noncomputable def rawPushPullMap {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁)
327	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
328	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ⟶
329	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₂).obj F) :=
330	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
331	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
332	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
333	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a p₁).hom.app
334	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫
335	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
336	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))) w) ≫
337	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
338	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₂).obj
339	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w)

342	/-- `pushPullMap` is the `Over X`-instance of `rawPushPullMap`. Holds by `rfl`. -/
343	lemma pushPullMap_eq_raw (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
344	    pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F :=

347	-- Composition law `pushPullMap_comp` is proved axiom-clean below (see `rawPushPullMap_comp`).
348	-- Dead-end note: `erw`/`congr 1` directly on `pullbackComp` whnf-unfolds it into its
349	-- `TwoSquare.equivNatTrans`/`mateEquiv` mate form, exploding heartbeats; the

354	coercions all collapse, leaving `(pushforward p₁).map` of the mate head
355	`η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom`. Project-local helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
356	lemma rawPushPullMap_self {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
357	    (F : X.Modules) :
358	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ (a ≫ p₁) rfl F =
359	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map

540	`O_X`-module nerve `CechNerve`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp` consumer). -/
541	noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
542	  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
543	  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
544	  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
545	  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
546	
547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
647	
648	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`
649	
650	The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
651	augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
652	over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
653	is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
654	counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
655	structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
656	un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
657	nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
658	pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/
659	
660	/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
661	alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
662	of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
663	`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
664	plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
665	exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
666	`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def cechComplexOnX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
668	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
669	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj
670	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))
671	
672	/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
673	object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
674	push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
675	isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
676	noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
677	    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
678	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F
680	
681	/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
682	augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
683	`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
684	`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
685	prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  -- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
703	  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
704	  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
705	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
706	      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
707	        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
708	    intro i
709	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
710	    simpa using h.symm
711	  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
712	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
713	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
714	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
715	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
716	  -- `erw` (defeq-matching) is needed: the `Comma`'s right functor `𝟭` pins the
717	  -- composition's middle object to `(𝟭).obj N.right`, so `comp_add`/`comp_neg`'s
718	  -- instances only match up to reducible defeq, not syntactically.
719	  erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]
720	
721	/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
722	`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
723	the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
724	cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
725	augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
726	alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
727	lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
728	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
729	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
730	  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
731	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
732	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
733	  rw [hd]
734	  erw [augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero (CechNerve 𝒰 F)]
735	  exact Limits.comp_zero
736	
737	/-- **The augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X`** (Stacks
738	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`). The cochain complex
739	`0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` obtained from the un-augmented {\v C}ech complex `cechComplexOnX`
740	by prepending the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰` (`cechAugmentation`) in degree `0` — so
741	`(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X 0 = F` and `(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X (p+1) = Cᵖ`. The
742	augmentation condition `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0` is `cechAugmentation_comp_d`. The lemma
743	`cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) asserts this complex is exact —
744	i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of `F` in `QCoh(X)`. Project-local. -/
745	noncomputable def cechAugmentedComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
746	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
747	  (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)
748	
749	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
750	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
751	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
752	
753	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
754	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
755	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
756	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
757	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
758	```
759	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.

/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N

instance : Category X.Modules where
  Hom := Modules.Hom
  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

section Functor

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

end Functor

variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}

section Presheaf

/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf

/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)

instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

variable (M) in
@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
  M.val.map_smul _ _ _

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  ext U x
  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M

@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)

@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
    Iso.hom_inv_id]
  simp

end Restriction

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

583	    (∏ᶜ fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (𝒰.f (σ k))) ≅

590	/- Planner strategy:
591	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
592	in `Over X`.
593	
594	Route:
595	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
596	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
597	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
598	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
599	
600	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
601	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
602	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
603	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
604	
605	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
606	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
607	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
608	
609	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the
610	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
611	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.
612	
613	Key Mathlib anchors:
614	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
615	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)
616	- `ColimitCocone` machinery for the coproduct in `Over X`
617	
618	Difficulty: MEDIUM — geometric bookkeeping, not sheaf theory. -/

622	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
623	  -- Universe reduction: reindex the cover index `𝒰.I₀ : Type u` to `Fin n : Type 0`, since the
624	  -- extensivity primitive `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (in `widePullback_coproduct_iso`) is Type-0-only.
625	  set n := Nat.card 𝒰.I₀

627	  let f' : (j : Fin n) → 𝒰.X (e.symm j) ⟶ X := fun j => 𝒰.f (e.symm j)

629	  let wZ : (∐ fun j : Fin n => 𝒰.X (e.symm j)) ≅ ∐ 𝒰.X :=

noncomputable def coprodOverIncl {ι : Type*} (legs : ι → Over X)
    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)] (i : ι) :
    legs i ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom)) :=
  Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => (legs i).left) i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])

/-- The canonical comparison map from the push–pull object on the coproduct of the legs to
the product of the per-leg push–pull objects: the `Pi.lift` of the push–pull maps of the
coproduct inclusions.  This is the canonical framing kept throughout the finite induction. -/
noncomputable def coprodToProdMap {ι : Type*} (F : X.Modules) (legs : ι → Over X)
    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)]
    [HasProduct (fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i))] :
    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom))) ⟶
      ∏ᶜ fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i) :=
  Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl legs i))

/-- A sheaf of modules over an empty (initial) scheme is a zero object.  All its sections
vanish: the structure sheaf has subsingleton sections over every open (`IsEmpty ↥Z`), so every
module of sections is subsingleton, and the underlying abelian presheaf is pointwise zero.  We
reflect `𝟙 = 0` through the faithful `toPresheaf`. -/

theorem unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) :
    adj1.unit ≫ whiskerRight (leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom G = adj2.unit := by
  ext x
  rw [NatTrans.comp_app, ← homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app adj1 adj2]
  simp

theorem unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app
    {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) (x : C) :
    adj1.unit.app x ≫ G.map ((leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom.app x) = adj2.unit.app x := by
  rw [← unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom adj1 adj2]; rfl

dsimp% (Augmented.drop.map f).app (op ⦋0⦌) ≫ Y.hom.app (op ⦋0⦌) =
      X.hom.app (op ⦋0⦌) ≫ Augmented.point.map f :=
  congr_app f.w (op ⦋0⦌)

variable (C)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Functor composition induces a functor on augmented simplicial objects. -/
@[simp]
def whiskeringObj (D : Type*) [Category* D] (F : C ⥤ D) : Augmented C ⥤ Augmented D where
  obj X :=
    { left := ((whiskering _ _).obj F).obj (drop.obj X)
      right := F.obj (point.obj X)
      hom := whiskerRight X.hom F ≫ (Functor.constComp _ _ _).hom }
  map η :=
    { left := whiskerRight η.left _
      right := F.map η.right
      w := by
        ext
        dsimp [whiskerRight]
        simp only [Category.comp_id, ← F.map_comp, ← NatTrans.comp_app]
        erw [η.w]
        rfl }

/-- Functor composition induces a functor on augmented simplicial objects. -/
@[simps]
def whiskering (D : Type u') [Category.{v'} D] : (C ⥤ D) ⥤ Augmented C ⥤ Augmented D where
  obj := whiskeringObj _ _
  map η :=
    { app := fun A =>
        { left := whiskerLeft _ η
          right := η.app _
          w := by
            ext n
            dsimp
            rw [Category.comp_id, Category.comp_id, η.naturality] } }
  map_comp := fun _ _ => by ext <;> rfl

variable {C}

/-- The constant augmented simplicial object functor. -/
@[simps]
def const : C ⥤ Augmented C where
  obj X :=
    { left := (SimplicialObject.const C).obj X
      right := X
      hom := 𝟙 _ }
  map f :=
    { left := (SimplicialObject.const C).map f
      right := f }

end Augmented

/-- Augment a simplicial object with an object. -/
@[simps]
def augment (X : SimplicialObject C) (X₀ : C) (f : X _⦋0⦌ ⟶ X₀)
    (w : ∀ (i : SimplexCategory) (g₁ g₂ : ⦋0⦌ ⟶ i),
      X.map g₁.op ≫ f = X.map g₂.op ≫ f) :
    SimplicialObject.Augmented C where
  left := X
  right := X₀
  hom :=
    { app := fun _ => X.map (SimplexCategory.const _ _ 0).op ≫ f
      naturality := by
        intro i j g
        dsimp
        rw [← g.op_unop]
        simpa only [← X.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, Category.comp_id, ← op_comp] using w _ _ _ }

-- Not `@[simp]` since `simp` can prove this.
theorem augment_hom_zero (X : SimplicialObject C) (X₀ : C) (f : X _⦋0⦌ ⟶ X₀) (w) :
    (X.augment X₀ f w).hom.app (op ⦋0⦌) = f := by simp

/-- The augmented simplicial object that is deduced from a simplicial object and
a terminal object. -/
@[simps!]
def augmentOfIsTerminal (X : SimplicialObject C) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    Augmented C where
  left := X
  right := T
  hom := { app _ := hT.from _ }

end SimplicialObject

/-- Cosimplicial objects. -/
def CosimplicialObject :=
  SimplexCategory ⥤ C

namespace CosimplicialObject

@[simps!]
        dsimp
        rw [← NatTrans.comp_app]
        erw [← η.w]
        rfl }

variable (C)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Functor composition induces a functor on augmented cosimplicial objects. -/
@[simp]
def whiskeringObj (D : Type*) [Category* D] (F : C ⥤ D) : Augmented C ⥤ Augmented D where
  obj X :=
    { left := F.obj (point.obj X)
      right := ((whiskering _ _).obj F).obj (drop.obj X)
      hom := (Functor.constComp _ _ _).inv ≫ whiskerRight X.hom F }
  map η :=
    { left := F.map η.left
      right := whiskerRight η.right _
      w := by
        ext
        dsimp
        rw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← F.map_comp, ← NatTrans.comp_app]
        erw [← η.w]
        rfl }

/-- Functor composition induces a functor on augmented cosimplicial objects. -/
@[simps]
def whiskering (D : Type u') [Category.{v'} D] : (C ⥤ D) ⥤ Augmented C ⥤ Augmented D where
  obj := whiskeringObj _ _
  map η :=
    { app := fun A =>
        { left := η.app _
          right := whiskerLeft _ η
          w := by
            ext n
            dsimp
            rw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, η.naturality] }
      naturality := fun _ _ f => by ext <;> simp }

variable {C}

/-- The constant augmented cosimplicial object functor. -/
@[simps]
def const : C ⥤ Augmented C where
  obj X :=
    { left := X
      right := (CosimplicialObject.const C).obj X
      hom := 𝟙 _ }
  map f :=
    { left := f
      right := (CosimplicialObject.const C).map f }

end Augmented

open Simplicial

/-- Augment a cosimplicial object with an object. -/
@[simps]
def augment (X : CosimplicialObject C) (X₀ : C) (f : X₀ ⟶ X.obj ⦋0⦌)
    (w : ∀ (i : SimplexCategory) (g₁ g₂ : ⦋0⦌ ⟶ i),
      f ≫ X.map g₁ = f ≫ X.map g₂) : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C where
  left := X₀
  right := X
  hom :=
    { app := fun _ => f ≫ X.map (SimplexCategory.const _ _ 0)
      naturality := by
        intro i j g
        dsimp
        rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, ← X.map_comp, w] }

-- Not `@[simp]` since `simp` can prove this.
theorem augment_hom_zero (X : CosimplicialObject C) (X₀ : C) (f : X₀ ⟶ X.obj ⦋0⦌) (w) :
    (X.augment X₀ f w).hom.app ⦋0⦌ = f := by simp

/-- The coaugmented cosimplicial object that is deduced from a cosimplicial object and
an initial object. -/
@[simps!]
def augmentOfIsInitial (X : CosimplicialObject C) {T : C} (hT : IsInitial T) :
    Augmented C where
  right := X
  left := T
  hom := { app _ := hT.to _ }

end CosimplicialObject

/-- The anti-equivalence between simplicial objects and cosimplicial objects. -/
@[simps!]
def simplicialCosimplicialEquiv : (SimplicialObject C)ᵒᵖ ≌ CosimplicialObject Cᵒᵖ :=
  Functor.leftOpRightOpEquiv _ _

/-- The anti-equivalence between cosimplicial objects and simplicial objects. -/
@[simps!]
def cosimplicialSimplicialEquiv : (CosimplicialObject C)ᵒᵖ ≌ SimplicialObject Cᵒᵖ :=
  Functor.opUnopEquiv _ _

/-- A component of a transposed form of the conjugation definition. -/
theorem conjugateEquiv_counit (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (d : D) :
    L₂.map ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
      α.app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
  simp

/-- A component of a transposed form of the inverse conjugation definition. -/
theorem conjugateEquiv_counit_symm (α : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (d : D) :
    L₂.map (α.app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
      ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
    exact (conjugateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)

/-- A component of a transposed form of the conjugation definition. -/
theorem unit_conjugateEquiv (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (c : C) :
    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _ =
      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) := by
  dsimp [conjugateEquiv]
  rw [id_comp, comp_id]
  have := unit_mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ (L₂.leftUnitor.hom ≫ α ≫ L₁.rightUnitor.inv) c
  dsimp at this
  rw [this]
  simp

/-- A component of a transposed form of the inverse conjugation definition. -/
theorem unit_conjugateEquiv_symm (α : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (c : C) :
    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ α.app _ =
      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _) := by
    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
    exact (unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) c)

@[simp]
theorem conjugateEquiv_id : conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₁ (𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _ := by
  ext
  simp

lemma Pi.map_π {f g : β → C} [HasProduct f] [HasProduct g] (p : ∀ b, f b ⟶ g b) (b : β) :
    Pi.map p ≫ Pi.π g b = Pi.π f b ≫ p b := by simp

@[simp]
lemma Pi.map_id {f : α → C} [HasProduct f] : Pi.map (fun a => 𝟙 (f a)) = 𝟙 (∏ᶜ f) := by
  ext; simp

lemma Pi.map_comp_map {f g h : α → C} [HasProduct f] [HasProduct g] [HasProduct h]
    G.obj (∏ᶜ f) ⟶ ∏ᶜ fun b => G.obj (f b) :=
  Pi.lift fun b => G.map (Pi.π f b)

@[reassoc (attr := simp), elementwise nosimp]
theorem piComparison_comp_π [HasProduct f] [HasProduct fun b => G.obj (f b)] (b : β) :
    piComparison G f ≫ Pi.π _ b = G.map (Pi.π f b) :=
  limit.lift_π _ (Discrete.mk b)

cat_disch

/-- Construct an isomorphism between categorical products (indexed by the same type)
from a family of isomorphisms between the factors.
-/
abbrev Pi.mapIso {f g : β → C} [HasProductsOfShape β C] (p : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) : ∏ᶜ f ≅ ∏ᶜ g :=
  lim.mapIso (Discrete.natIso fun X => p X.as)

instance Pi.map_isIso {f g : β → C} [HasProductsOfShape β C] (p : ∀ b, f b ⟶ g b)
    [∀ b, IsIso <| p b] : IsIso <| Pi.map p :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso (Pi.mapIso (fun b ↦ asIso (p b))).hom)

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationBase
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sub-brick A — Leg: the `coreIso_comm` chain
10	
11	The `coreIso_comm` chain (`abHom_finsetSum_apply`, `coreIso_comm_leg`, `coreIso_comm_coface`,
12	`coreIso_comm_sum`, `coreIso_comm`) expressing naturality of the degreewise section iso
13	through the Čech differentials. Depends on `CechSectionIdentificationBase`.
14	
15	Carries the residual sorry `coreIso_comm_leg`.
16	-/
17	
18	universe u
19	
20	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
21	
22	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
23	
24	open Scheme.Modules
25	
26	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
27	
28	/-! ### The `coreIso_comm` chain (`lem:coreIso_comm_leg` → `lem:coreIso_comm_coface` →
29	`lem:coreIso_comm_sum` → `lem:coreIso_comm`), built bottom-up per the iter-072 effort-break. -/
30	
31	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum.  (Local copy of
32	the `CechAcyclic` private helper `ab_hom_finsetSum_apply`.) -/
33	private lemma abHom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
34	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
35	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
36	  classical
37	  induction s using Finset.induction with
38	  | empty => simp
39	  | insert a s ha ih =>
40	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
41	
42	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
43	/-- **Per-leg naturality of the core comparison coface** (`lem:coreIso_comm_leg`).
44	For a fixed coface index `k` and multi-index `σ'`, the `σ'`-coordinate (the projection
45	`Pi.π … σ'`) of the evaluated push–pull coface `G_V(Ψ(δ^nerve_k))` followed by the
46	degree-`(p+1)` object iso equals the presheaf face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr σ' k`
47	applied to the `(σ' ∘ d_k)`-coordinate of the degree-`p` object iso.  This is the genuine
48	geometric unwinding of `coreIso_objIso` through `pushPull_eval_prod_iso`,
49	`pushPull_sigma_iso`, the product-leg projection, and `pushPull_leg_sections`. -/
50	lemma coreIso_comm_leg (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
51	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (p : ℕ) (k : Fin (p + 2)) (σ' : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) :
52	    (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
53	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
54	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
55	            PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
56	          ((CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ k)) ≫
57	        (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom ≫
58	        Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
59	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
60	            (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ' =
61	      (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom ≫
62	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
63	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
64	            (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (τ l) ⊓ V))))
65	          (σ' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ k).toOrderHom) ≫
66	        sectionCechFaceRestr (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
67	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) σ' k := by
68	  sorry

71	/-- **Per-coface square of the core comparison** (`lem:coreIso_comm_coface`): for each
72	degree `p` and coface index `k`, the object isos intertwine the individual cofaces.
73	Coordinatewise extensionality (`Pi.hom_ext`); the `σ'`-coordinate of the left side is the
74	face restriction by the defining `Pi.lift_π` of the section-Čech cosimplicial map, and the
75	right side is `coreIso_comm_leg`. -/
76	lemma coreIso_comm_coface (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
77	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (p : ℕ) (k : Fin (p + 2)) :
78	    (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom ≫
79	        (sectionCechCosimplicial (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
80	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F)).δ k =
81	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
82	            (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
83	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
84	              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
85	            ((CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ k)) ≫
86	        (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom := by
87	  ext x
88	  apply (sectionCechProductEquiv (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
89	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) (p + 1)).injective
90	  funext σ'
91	  have hπ : (sectionCechCosimplicial (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
92	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F)).map (SimplexCategory.δ k) ≫
93	        Pi.π _ σ' =
94	      Pi.π _ (σ' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ k).toOrderHom) ≫
95	        sectionCechFaceRestr (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
96	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) σ' k :=
97	    Pi.lift_π _ σ'
98	  have hL := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom hπ (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom x)
99	  have hR := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (coreIso_comm_leg 𝒰 F V p k σ') x
100	  exact ((sectionCechProductEquiv_apply (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
101	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) (p + 1) _ σ').trans hL).trans
102	    (((sectionCechProductEquiv_apply (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
103	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) (p + 1) _ σ').trans hR).symm)
104

106	/-- **Alternating-sum assembly of the core comparison square** (`lem:coreIso_comm_sum`):
107	the full alternating-coface differentials are intertwined by the object isos.  Proved
108	ELEMENTWISE (per the iter-067 dead-end note: no `Preadditive.comp_sum` against the bundled
109	`AddCommGrpCat`-hom `objD`): both sides, evaluated at an element and a coordinate `σ'`, are
110	the same finite alternating sum, matched summand-by-summand by `coreIso_comm_leg`. -/
111	lemma coreIso_comm_sum (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
112	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (p : ℕ) :
113	    (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom ≫
114	        AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
115	          (sectionCechCosimplicial (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
116	            ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F)) p =
117	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
118	            (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
119	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
120	              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
121	            (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
122	              (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) p)) ≫
123	        (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom := by
124	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
125	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
126	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
127	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).Additive :=
128	    inferInstance
129	  -- (1) Element-level decomposition of the evaluated nerve differential.  All steps are
130	  -- term-chained (`Eq.trans`/`congrArg`) — no `rw` of a `have` against the goal, dodging the
131	  -- instance-path mismatch on the `Finset.sum` of the `Preadditive` hom group.
132	  have hpush : ∀ x : ToType (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
133	        (pushPullObj F
134	          ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
135	        (Opposite.op V)),
136	      ConcreteCategory.hom
137	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
138	              (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
139	            ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
140	                PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
141	              (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
142	                (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) p))) x =
143	        ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
144	          ConcreteCategory.hom
145	            ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
146	                (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
147	              ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
148	                  PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
149	                ((CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i))) x := by
150	    intro x
151	    have h1 : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
152	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
153	          (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
154	            (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) p) =
155	        ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
156	            PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
157	          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i) :=
158	      Functor.map_sum _ _ _
159	    have h2 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
160	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
161	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
162	              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
163	            (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
164	              (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) p)) =
165	        ∑ i : Fin (p + 2),
166	          (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
167	              (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
168	            ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
169	                PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
170	              ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
171	                (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i)) :=
172	      (congrArg (fun m => (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
173	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj
174	            (Opposite.op V)).map m) h1).trans
175	        (Functor.map_sum _ _ _)
176	    refine (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom h2 x).trans ?_
177	    rw [abHom_finsetSum_apply]
178	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
179	    have h3 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
180	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
181	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
182	              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
183	            ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
184	              (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i)) =
185	        (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
186	          (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
187	              (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
188	            ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
189	                PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
190	              ((CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i)) :=
191	      (congrArg (fun m => (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
192	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj
193	            (Opposite.op V)).map m)
194	        (Functor.map_zsmul _)).trans (Functor.map_zsmul _)
195	    exact (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom h3 x).trans rfl
196	  -- (2) Elementwise comparison through the product equivalence, coordinate by coordinate.
197	  ext x
198	  apply (sectionCechProductEquiv (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
199	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) (p + 1)).injective
200	  funext σ'
201	  refine Eq.trans (sectionCech_objD_apply (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
202	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) p
203	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom x) σ') (Eq.symm ?_)
204	  refine Eq.trans (sectionCechProductEquiv_apply (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
205	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) (p + 1) _ σ') ?_
206	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
207	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
208	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
209	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ'))
210	      (ConcreteCategory.comp_apply _ ((coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom) x)) ?_
211	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
212	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
213	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
214	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ'))
215	      (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom) (hpush x))) ?_
216	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
217	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
218	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
219	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ'))
220	      (map_sum (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom) _ Finset.univ)) ?_
221	  refine Eq.trans (map_sum (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
222	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
223	        (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ')) _ Finset.univ) ?_
224	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun i _ => ?_
225	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg
226	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
227	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
228	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ'))
229	      (map_zsmul (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F (p + 1) V).hom) _ _)) ?_
230	  refine Eq.trans (map_zsmul (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
231	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
232	        (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ l) ⊓ V)))) σ')) _ _) ?_
233	  congr 1
234	  have hleg := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (coreIso_comm_leg 𝒰 F V p i σ') x
235	  simp only [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply] at hleg
236	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
237	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F) p
238	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V).hom x)
239	    (σ' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)]
240	  exact hleg

242	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
243	/-- **The core comparison intertwines the Čech differentials** (`lem:coreIso_comm`).  Under the
244	degreewise object isos `coreIso_objIso`, the alternating-coface differential of the evaluated
245	backbone complex `(G_V ∘ Ψ) Č•(𝒰, F)` matches the alternating-coface differential of the
246	concrete restricted section complex `Č•(𝒰', F)`.  The square is exactly the alternating-sum
247	assembly `coreIso_comm_sum` (built from the per-coface squares `coreIso_comm_coface`, in turn
248	from the per-leg naturality `coreIso_comm_leg`). -/
249	lemma coreIso_comm (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
250	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i j : ℕ) (hij : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).Rel i j) :
251	    (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F i V).hom ≫ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).d i j =
252	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
253	            (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj
254	              (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
255	          (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
256	              PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
257	            (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))).d i j ≫
258	        (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F j V).hom := by
259	  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
260	  rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d]
261	  have hsec : (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).d i (i + 1) =
262	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
263	        (sectionCechCosimplicial (fun a => coverOpen 𝒰 a ⊓ V)
264	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F)) i :=
265	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) i
266	  have hX : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d i (i + 1) =
267	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
268	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) i :=
269	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) i
270	  rw [hsec, hX]
271	  exact coreIso_comm_sum 𝒰 F V i
272	
273	end AlgebraicGeometry

352	/-- Clean (transport-free) form of `rawPushPullMap` when the over-triangle is `rfl`
353	(`p₂ = a ≫ p₁`): the pushforward comparison `pushforwardComp` and the `eqToHom`
354	coercions all collapse, leaving `(pushforward p₁).map` of the mate head
355	`η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom`. Project-local helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
356	lemma rawPushPullMap_self {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
357	    (F : X.Modules) :
358	    rawPushPullMap a p₁ (a ≫ p₁) rfl F =
359	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
360	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
361	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
362	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
363	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) := by
364	  simp only [rawPushPullMap, pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp,
365	    Category.comp_id, Functor.map_comp]
366	  rfl

369	/-- Clean form of `rawPushPullMap` for a general over-triangle `w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂`:
370	the transport-free head `(pushforward p₁).map (η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom)`
371	followed by the single `eqToHom` identifying the target along `w`. Project-local
372	helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
373	lemma rawPushPullMap_self_gen {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X)
374	    (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :

376	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
377	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
378	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
379	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
380	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F)) ≫
381	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward q).obj
382	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) w) := by
383	  subst w
384	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self]
385	  exact (Category.comp_id _).symm

387	/-- The pure pullback **pentagon** at `F`, in transport-light form: the content of
388	`pushPullMap_comp` once the units and pushforwards are peeled. It is exactly
389	`Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)` evaluated at
390	`F`, with the associator (an identity on components) absorbed and the two leading
391	comparison isos inverted. Project-local. -/
392	lemma pushPull_pentagon {Z₁ Z₂ Z₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁) (b : Z₃ ⟶ Z₂)
393	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (F : X.Modules) :
394	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
395	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F ≫
396	        eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)
397	          (Category.assoc b a p₁)) =
398	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
399	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F := by
400	  have H := Scheme.Modules.pseudofunctor_associativity (f := b) (g := a) (h := p₁)
401	  have HF := congrArg (fun t => NatTrans.app t F) H
402	  simp only [NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
403	    Functor.associator_hom_app, eqToHom_app, Functor.comp_obj,
404	    Category.id_comp] at HF
405	  -- HF : A1⁻¹ ≫ B1⁻¹ ≫ C ≫ D = eqToHom eF, with the associator (= 𝟙) absorbed.
406	  -- Cancel the two leading isos `B1 = (pullback b).map (pullbackComp a p₁).hom`, `A1 = pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)`
407	  -- against their inverses (no `IsIso` instances needed: `Iso.hom_inv_id_app` + functoriality),
408	  -- then feed `HF` in via `congrArg`/`trans` (defeq, so no fragile `rw [HF]` matching).
409	  have cancel : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
410	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
411	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F ≫
412	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) ≫
413	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
414	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F =
415	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F) ≫
416	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F := by
417	    have h1 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫
418	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).inv.app F = 𝟙 _ := Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _
419	    have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).inv.app F) = 𝟙 _ :=
421	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
422	        ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)).trans
423	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map_id _))
424	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj] at h1 h2 ⊢
425	    rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]
426	  have hcd := cancel.symm.trans (congrArg (fun t => (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
427	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a p₁).hom.app F) ≫
428	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b (a ≫ p₁)).hom.app F ≫ t) HF)
429	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app
430	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F))
431	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (b ≫ a) p₁).hom.app F), hcd]
432	  simp [eqToHom_trans]

noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
  map_id n := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
    simp
  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
    apply Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
    congr 1
